Batch-corrected SummarizedExperiment object with QC sample assays and internal standard ratio data

data_out.description: Recommendation table (tab-delimited or CSV) with columns: compound, recommended_internal_standard, min_RSDQC
Full RSDQC matrix (tab-delimited or CSV) with compounds as rows and internal standard candidates as columns, showing RSDQC for each pair

domain_knowledge: RSDQC (Relative Standard Deviation of QC samples) is computed on batch-corrected compound/internal standard ratios and measures consistency of ionization and detector response across repeated QC injections, with lower values indicating more stable internal standards.
An internal standard is optimal for a given compound when its RSDQC is minimized, indicating that the compound-to-standard ratio exhibits the least variability after batch correction.
Batch correction using pooled study quality control samples (SQC) adjusts for instrument drift and matrix effects; RSDQC must be calculated post-correction to reflect true analytical stability.

The exhaustive evaluation across all internal standard candidates per compound acknowledges that no single internal standard performs equally well for all analytes due to differential ionization suppression and detector response.

verify output_matches_reference against reported recommendation table location in article (if deposited), or verify table structure is consistent with mzQuality export format
expert_review: verify that RSDQC calculation methodology matches article description: relative standard deviation computed over batch-corrected QC sample ratios (compound / internal standard) for each candidate pairing
verify that the lowest RSDQC selection logic is correctly applied per compound (no ties, handling of missing values, numerical precision)
assess whether batch-correction step (using pooled SQC samples) was applied before RSDQC computation, as stated in article scope

[methods] Batch-corrected SummarizedExperiment object with QC sample assays and internal standard ratio data: 'Build SummarizedExperiment object from data frame with specified columns for compounds, samples, and assays'

[methods] Recommendation table (tab-delimited or CSV) with columns: compound, recommended_internal_standard, min_RSDQC: 'mzQuality is capable of recommending different internal standards by exhaustively calculating the Relative Standard Deviation of QC samples (RSDQC) of batch-corrected ratio's.'

[methods] Full RSDQC matrix (tab-delimited or CSV) with compounds as rows and internal standard candidates as columns, showing RSDQC for each pair: 'an export will be made that contains the mentioned plots, tab-delimited text files for programmatic access'
[methods] R: 'mzQuality is a user-friendly R package'
[methods] mzQuality: 'mzQuality is capable of recommending different internal standards by exhaustively calculating the Relative Standard Deviation of QC samples (RSDQC)'
[methods] SummarizedExperiment: 'Internally, mzQuality uses Bioconductors' *SummarizedExperiment* object to store the data.'

cmd: library(SummarizedExperiment); library(mzQuality); se <- readRDS('{inputs.batch_corrected_experiment}'); qc_idx <- grep('QC', colData(se)$sample_type, ignore.case=TRUE); compounds <- rownames(se); int_stds <- unique(unlist(strsplit(colnames(assay(se, 'ratio_corrected')), '_'))); rsdqc_matrix <- matrix(NA, nrow=length(compounds), ncol=length(int_stds), dimnames=list(compounds, int_stds)); for(i in seq_along(compounds)) { for(j in seq_along(int_stds)) { col_pattern <- paste0(compounds[i], '_', int_stds[j]); matching_cols <- grep(col_pattern, colnames(assay(se, 'ratio_corrected')), fixed=TRUE); if(length(matching_cols) > 0) { qc_vals <- assay(se, 'ratio_corrected')[i, intersect(matching_cols, qc_idx)]; if(length(qc_vals) > 1 && !all(is.na(qc_vals))) { rsdqc_matrix[i,j] <- (sd(qc_vals, na.rm=TRUE) / mean(qc_vals, na.rm=TRUE)) * 100; } } } }; recommendation <- data.frame(compound=rownames(rsdqc_matrix), recommended_internal_standard=colnames(rsdqc_matrix)[apply(rsdqc_matrix, 1, which.min)], min_RSDQC=apply(rsdqc_matrix, 1, min, na.rm=TRUE)); write.table(recommendation, 'task_003_recommendation.tsv', sep='\t', row.names=FALSE, quote=FALSE); write.table(rsdqc_matrix, 'task_003_rsdqc_matrix.tsv', sep='\t', quote=FALSE);

task_description: Extract relative standard deviation of QC samples (RSDQC) values for each compound across all internal standard candidates from a batch-corrected experiment object, and identify the internal standard achieving the lowest RSDQC per compound. Produce a recommendation table.
task_objective: Extract RSDQC values for all internal standard candidates per compound from batch-corrected experiment and produce a recommendation table identifying the optimal internal standard for each compound.

Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: SummarizedExperiment, Recommendation table (tab-delimited or CSV) with columns: compound, recommended_internal_standard, min_RSDQC, Full RSDQC matrix (tab-delimited or CSV) with compounds as rows and internal standard candidates as columns, showing RSDQC for each pair.
workflow_description: 1. Load the batch-corrected SummarizedExperiment object containing QC sample ratios (compound / internal standard) and their batch-correction metadata. 2. For each compound, iterate over all internal standard candidates and compute the relative standard deviation (RSD) of the batch-corrected QC sample ratios. 3. Tabulate RSDQC values in a matrix with compounds as rows and internal standards as columns. 4. For each compound, identify the internal standard with the minimum RSDQC value. 5. Construct and export a recommendation table listing each compound, its recommended internal standard, and the corresponding minimum RSDQC.
